concept: How about an action game where my character is made of thousands of tiny robots that can split apart and reconfigure to overcome obstacles? I could flow through small openings, form bridges across gaps, create tools for specific situations, or split into multiple smaller characters to operate mechanisms simultaneously. Different environmental hazards affect my swarm differently - magnets disrupt formation, water slows movement, electricity can stun portions of the collective. The narrative explores themes of individual versus collective identity as the swarm gradually develops distributed consciousness rather than central control.
